step_result.step.output_file.exists():\n    96→            changed.add(step_result.step.output_file)\n    97→        return changed\n    98→\n    99→\n   100→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   101→# 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n   102→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   103→\n   104→\n   105→@dataclass\n   106→class IsolationLayers:\n   107→    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n   108→\n   109→    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n   110→    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n   111→    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n   112→    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n   113→    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n   114→\n   115→    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n   116→    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n   117→    \"\"\"\n   118→\n   119→    scoped_work_dir: Path\n   120→    git_boundary: Path\n   121→    plugin_dir: Path\n   122→    settings_dir: Path\n   123→\n   124→    @property\n   125→    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n   126→        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n   127→        return {\n   128→            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(self.scoped_work_dir),\n   129→            \"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\": str(self.git_boundary),\n   130→            \"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\": str(self.plugin_dir),\n   131→            \"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\": str(self.settings_dir),\n   132→        }\n   133→\n   134→    @property\n   135→    def layers_active(self) -> list[str]:\n   136→        \"\"\"Return list of active isolation layer names for verification.\"\"\"\n   137→        active = []\n   138→        if self.scoped_work_dir.exists():\n   139→            active.append(\"scoped_work_dir\")\n   140→        if self.git_boundary.exists():\n   141→            active.append(\"git_boundary\")\n   142→        if self.plugin_dir.exists():\n   143→            active.append(\"empty_plugin_dir\")\n   144→        if self.settings_dir.exists():\n   145→            active.append(\"restricted_settings\")\n   146→        return active\n   147→\n   148→\n   149→def setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n   150→    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n   151→\n   152→    Sets up:\n   153→    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n   154→    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n   155→    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n   156→    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n   157→\n   158→    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n   159→    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n   160→\n   161→    Args:\n   162→        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n   163→\n   164→    Returns:\n   165→        IsolationLayers with all 4 layers configured.\n   166→    \"\"\"\n   167→    base = config.results_dir / \".isolation\"\n   168→    base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   169→\n   170→    plugin_dir = base / \"plugins\"\n   171→    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   172→\n   173→    settings_dir = base / \"settings\"\n   174→    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   175→\n   176→    return IsolationLayers(\n   177→        scoped_work_dir=config.release_dir,\n   178→        git_boundary=config.release_dir,\n   179→        plugin_dir=plugin_dir,\n   180→        settings_dir=settings_dir,\n   181→    )\n   182→\n   183→\n   184→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   185→# Result Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n   186→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   187→\n   188→\n   189→@dataclass\n   190→class AggregatedPhaseReport:\n   191→    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n   192→\n   193→    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n   194→    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n   195→    are budget-exhausted.\n   196→    \"\"\"\n   197→\n   198→    phase_number: int\n   199→    tasks_total: int = 0\n   200→    tasks_passed: int = 0\n   201→    tasks_failed: int = 0\n   202→    tasks_incomplete: int = 0\n   203→    tasks_skipped: int = 0\n   204→    tasks_not_attempted: int = 0\n   205→    budget_remaining: int = 0\n   206→    total_turns_consumed: int = 0\n   207→    total_duration_seconds: float = 0.0\n   208→    task_results: list[TaskResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n   209→    remaining_task_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n   210→\n   211→    @property\n   212→    def status(self) -> str:\n   213→        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n   214→        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n   215→            return \"PASS\"\n   216→        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n   217→            return \"PASS\"\n   218→        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n   219→            return \"FAIL\"\n   220→        return \"PARTIAL\"\n   221→\n   222→    def to_yaml(self) -> str:\n   223→        \"\"\"Render the report as a YAML string.\n   224→\n   225→        Produces a machine-readable YAML document with standardized fields\n   226→        for downstream tooling and TUI display.\n   227→        \"\"\"\n   228→        lines = [\n   229→            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n   230→            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n   231→            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n   232→            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n   233→            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n   234→            f\"tasks_incomplete: {self.tasks_incomplete}\",\n   235→            f\"tasks_not_attempted: {self.tasks_not_attempted}\",\n   236→            f\"budget_remaining: {self.budget_remaining}\",\n   237→            f\"total_turns_consumed: {self.total_turns_consumed}\",\n   238→            f\"total_duration_seconds: {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}\",\n   239→            \"tasks:\",\n   240→        ]\n   241→        for tr in self.task_results:\n   242→            lines.append(f\"  - task_id: {tr.task.task_id}\")\n   243→            lines.append(f'    title: \"{tr.task.title}\"')\n   244→            lines.append(f\"    status: {tr.status.value}\")\n   245→            lines.append(f\"    gate_outcome: {tr.gate_outcome.value}\")\n   246→            lines.append(f\"    turns_consumed: {tr.turns_consumed}\")\n   247→            lines.append(f\"    duration_seconds: {tr.duration_seconds:.1f}\")\n   248→        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n   249→            lines.append(\"remaining_tasks:\")\n   250→            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n   251→                lines.append(f\"  - {tid}\")\n   252→        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n   253→\n   254→    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n   255→        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n   256→        lines = [\n   257→            \"---\",\n   258→            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n   259→            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n   260→            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n   261→            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n   262→            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n   263→            \"---\",\n   264→            \"\",\n   265→            f\"# Phase {self.phase_number} — Aggregated Task Report\",\n   266→            \"\",\n   267→            \"| Task ID | Title | Status | Turns | Duration |\",\n   268→            \"|---------|-------|--------|-------|----------|\",\n   269→        ]\n   270→        for tr in self.task_results:\n   271→            dur = f\"{tr.duration_seconds:.1f}s\"\n   272→            lines.append(\n   273→                f\"| {tr.task.task_id} | {tr.task.title} | {tr.status.value} \"\n   274→                f\"| {tr.turns_consumed} | {dur} |\"\n   275→            )\n   276→        lines.append(\"\")\n   277→        lines.append(f\"**Total turns consumed:** {self.total_turns_consumed}\")\n   278→        lines.append(f\"**Total duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n   279→\n   280→        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n   281→            lines.append(\"\")\n   282→            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n   283→            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n   284→                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n   285→\n   286→        lines.append(\"\")\n   287→        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n   288→            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n   289→        else:\n   290→            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n   291→\n   292→        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→def aggregate_task_results(\n   296→    phase_number: int,\n   297→    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n   298→    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n   299→    budget_remaining: int = 0,\n   300→) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n   301→    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n   302→\n   303→    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n   304→    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n   305→\n   306→    Args:\n   307→        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n   308→        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n   309→        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n   310→\n   311→    Returns:\n   312→        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n   313→    \"\"\"\n   314→    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n   315→        phase_number=phase_number,\n   316→        task_results=task_results,\n   317→        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n   318→        budget_remaining=budget_remaining,\n   319→    )\n   320→\n   321→    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n   322→    report.tasks_passed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS)\n   323→    report.tasks_failed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL)\n   324→    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n   325→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   326→    )\n   327→    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n   328→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n   329→    )\n   330→    report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n   331→    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n   332→    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(r.duration_seconds for r in task_results)\n   333→\n   334→    return report\n   335→\n   336→\n   337→def check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n   338→    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n   339→\n   340→    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n   341→    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n   342→    \"\"\"\n   343→    if ledger is None:\n   344→        return None\n   345→    if ledger.can_launch():\n   346→        return None\n   347→    return (\n   348→        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n   349→        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n   350→    )\n   351→\n   352→\n   353→def run_wiring_safeguard_checks(\n   354→    config: SprintConfig,\n   355→    report: object | None = None,\n   356→) -> list[str]:\n   357→    \"\"\"Pre-activation safeguards for wiring analysis (SC-010, section 7 Phase 1).\n   358→\n   359→    Checks run before the first wiring analysis in a sprint session:\n   360→    1. Zero-match warning: >50 files scanned but 0 findings → suspicious\n   361→    2. Whitelist validation: attempt to load whitelist, warn if parse fails\n   362→    3. provider_dir_names check: verify configured directories exist\n   363→\n   364→    Safeguards produce warnings only; they never block sprint execution.\n   365→\n   366→    Returns a list of warning messages (empty if all checks pass).\n   367→    \"\"\"\n   368→    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig, load_whitelist\n   369→\n   370→    warnings: list[str] = []\n   371→\n   372→    # Check 1: Zero-match warning (requires a report from a prior run)\n   373→    if report is not None:\n   374→        files_scanned = getattr(report, \"files_analyzed\", 0)\n   375→        total_findings = getattr(report, \"total_findings\", 0)\n   376→        if files_scanned > 50 and total_findings == 0:\n   377→            msg = (\n   378→                f\"Zero-match warning: {files_scanned} files scanned but \"\n   379→                f\"0 findings produced — verify wiring analysis configuration\"\n   380→            )\n   381→            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n   382→            warnings.append(msg)\n   383→\n   384→    # Check 2: Whitelist validation\n   385→    # Try to parse with \"soft\" mode so malformed YAML raises instead of silently\n   386→    # returning empty. This is a safeguard check, not runtime behavior.\n   387→    whitelist_path = config.release_dir / \"src\" / \"superclaude\" / \"cli\" / \"audit\" / \"wiring_whitelist.yaml\"\n   388→    if whitelist_path.exists():\n   389→        try:\n   390→            import yaml as _yaml\n   391→            raw = _yaml.safe_load(whitelist_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n   392→            if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n   393→                msg = f\"Whitelist validation failed: file at {whitelist_path} is not a YAML mapping\"\n   394→                _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n   395→                warnings.append(msg)\n   396→        except Exception as exc:\n   397→            msg = f\"Whitelist validation failed: {exc}\"\n   398→            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n   399→            warnings.append(msg)\n   400→\n   401→    # Check 3: provider_dir_names sanity check\n   402→    wiring_config = WiringConfig()\n   403→    source_dir = config.release_dir\n   404→    for dir_name in wiring_config.provider_dir_names:\n   405→        found = list(source_dir.rglob(dir_name))\n   406→        dirs_found = [d for d in found if d.is_dir()]\n   407→        if not dirs_found:\n   408→            msg = (\n   409→                f\"Provider directory '{dir_name}' not found under \"\n   410→                f\"{source_dir} — orphan module detection may miss files\"\n   411→            )\n   412→            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n   413→            warnings.append(msg)\n   414→\n   415→    return warnings\n   416→\n   417→\n   418→def run_post_task_wiring_hook(\n   419→    task: TaskEntry,\n   420→    config: SprintConfig,\n   421→    task_result: TaskResult,\n   422→) -> TaskResult:\n   423→    \"\"\"Run post-task wiring analysis based on config.wiring_gate_mode.\n   424→\n   425→    Mode behavior:\n   426→    - off: skip analysis entirely, return task_result unchanged\n   427→    - shadow: run analysis, log findings, task status unchanged (SC-006)\n   428→    - soft: run analysis, warn on critical findings, task status unchanged\n   429→    - full: run analysis, block (set FAIL) on critical+major findings\n   430→\n   431→    Returns the (possibly modified) TaskResult. Only full mode may change\n   432→    task status to FAIL when blocking findings exist.\n   433→    \"\"\"\n   434→    mode = config.wiring_gate_mode\n   435→\n   436→    if mode == \"off\":\n   437→        return task_result\n   438→\n   439→    # Lazy import to avoid circular dependency at module level\n   440→    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_gate import run_wiring_analysis, WiringReport\n   441→    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig\n   442→\n   443→    source_dir = config.release_dir\n   444→    wiring_config = WiringConfig(rollout_mode=\"shadow\" if mode == \"off\" else mode if mode in (\"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\") else \"shadow\")\n   445→\n   446→    try:\n   447→        report = run_wiring_analysis(wiring_config, source_dir)\n   448→    except Exception as exc:\n   449→        _wiring_logger.warning(\n   450→            \"Wiring analysis failed for task %s: %s — continuing\",\n   451→            task.task_id,\n   452→            exc,\n   453→        )\n   454→        return task_result\n   455→\n   456→    total = report.total_findings\n   457→    blocking = report.blocking_count(mode)\n   458→\n   459→    _wiring_logger.info(\n   460→        \"Wiring hook [%s] task=%s: %d findings, %d blocking (%.4fs)\",\n   461→        mode,\n   462→        task.task_id,\n   463→        total,\n   464→        blocking,\n   465→        report.scan_duration_seconds,\n   466→    )\n   467→\n   468→    if mode == \"shadow\":\n   469→        # SC-006: log findings without affecting task status\n   470→        if total > 0:\n   471→            _wiring_logger.info(\n   472→                \"Shadow mode: %d findings logged for task %s (status unchanged)\",\n   473→                total,\n   474→                task.task_id,\n   475→            )\n   476→        return task_result\n   477→\n   478→    if mode == \"soft\":\n   479→        # Warn on critical findings but don't change status\n   480→        critical_count = sum(\n   481→            1 for f in report.unsuppressed_findings if f.severity == \"critical\"\n   482→        )\n   483→        if critical_count > 0:\n   484→            _wiring_logger.warning(\n   485→                \"Soft mode: %d critical findings for task %s\",\n   486→                critical_count,\n   487→                task.task_id,\n   488→            )\n   489→        return task_result\n   490→\n   491→    if mode == \"full\":\n   492→        # Block on critical+major findings\n   493→        if blocking > 0:\n   494→            _wiring_logger.error(\n   495→                \"Full mode: %d blocking findings for task %s — marking FAIL\",\n   496→                blocking,\n   497→                task.task_id,\n   498→            )\n   499→            task_result.status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   500→            task_result.gate_outcome = GateOutcome.FAIL\n   501→        return task_result\n   502→\n   503→    return task_result\n   504→\n   505→\n   506→def execute_phase_tasks(\n   507→    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   508→    config: SprintConfig,\n   509→    phase,\n   510→    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   511→    *,\n   512→    _subprocess_factory=None,\n   513→) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n   514→    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n   515→\n   516→    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n   517→    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n   518→    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n   519→\n   520→    Args:\n   521→        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n   522→        config: Sprint configuration.\n   523→        phase: The Phase being executed.\n   524→        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n   525→        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n   526→            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n   527→\n   528→    Returns:\n   529→        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n   530→        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n   531→    \"\"\"\n   532→    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n   533→    remaining: list[str] = []\n   534→\n   535→    if not tasks:\n   536→        return results, remaining\n   537→\n   538→    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n   539→        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   540→\n   541→        # Budget check: can we launch?\n   542→        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n   543→            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n   544→            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n   545→            for t in tasks[i:]:\n   546→                results.append(\n   547→                    TaskResult(\n   548→                        task=t,\n   549→                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n   550→                        started_at=started_at,\n   551→                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   552→                    )\n   553→                )\n   554→            break\n   555→\n   556→        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n   557→        if ledger is not None:\n   558→            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n   559→\n   560→        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n   561→        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n   562→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n   563→                task, config, phase\n   564→            )\n   565→        else:\n   566→            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n   567→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n   568→                task, config, phase\n   569→            )\n   570→\n   571→        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   572→\n   573→        # Determine task status from exit code\n   574→        if exit_code == 0:\n   575→            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n   576→        elif exit_code == 124:\n   577→            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   578→        else:\n   579→            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   580→\n   581→        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n   582→        if ledger is not None:\n   583→            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n   584→            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n   585→            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n   586→            if actual > pre_allocated:\n   587→                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n   588→            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n   589→                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n   590→\n   591→        result = TaskResult(\n   592→            task=task,\n   593→            status=status,\n   594→            turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n   595→            exit_code=exit_code,\n   596→            started_at=started_at,\n   597→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   598→            output_bytes=output_bytes,\n   599→        )\n   600→\n   601→        # Post-task wiring hook: run wiring analysis per config.wiring_gate_mode\n   602→        result = run_post_task_wiring_hook(task, config, result)\n   603→\n   604→        results.append(result)\n   605→\n   606→    return results, remaining\n   607→\n   608→\n   609→def _run_task_subprocess(\n   610→    task: TaskEntry,\n   611→    config: SprintConfig,\n   612→    phase,\n   613→) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n   614→    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n   615→\n   616→    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n   617→    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n   618→    \"\"\"\n   619→    # Build a task-specific prompt\n   620→    prompt = (\n   621→        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n   622→        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n   623→        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n   624→    )\n   625→\n   626→    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n   627→    proc.config = config\n   628→    proc.phase = phase\n   629→    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n   630→\n   631→    _Base.__init__(\n   632→        proc,\n   633→        prompt=prompt,\n   634→        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   635→        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   636→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   637→        model=config.model,\n   638→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   639→        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   640→        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   641→    )\n   642→    proc.start()\n   643→    proc.wait()\n   644→    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n   645→    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   646→    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n   647→    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n   648→    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n   649→\n   650→\n   651→def execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n   652→    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n   653→\n   654→    For each active phase:\n   655→    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n   656→    2. Start output monitor thread\n   657→    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n   658→    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n   659→    5. Record PhaseResult\n   660→    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n   661→    \"\"\"\n   662→    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n   663→    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n   664→        raise SystemExit(\n   665→            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n   666→            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n   667→        )\n   668→\n   669→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   670→    signal_handler.install()\n   671→\n   672→    setup_debug_logger(config)\n   673→    import logging as _logging\n   674→\n   675→    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n   676→\n   677→    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n   678→    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n   679→    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n   680→    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n   681→\n   682→    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n   683→    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n   684→\n   685→    tui.start()\n   686→\n   687→    # Startup orphan cleanup: remove stale isolation dirs from crashed previous runs\n   688→    shutil.rmtree(config.results_dir / \".isolation\", ignore_errors=True)\n   689→\n   690→    # Execute all python-mode phases via preflight executor before the main loop.\n   691→    # Removing this single call reverts to all-Claude behavior (R-051 rollback property).\n   692→    # Lazy import breaks the preflight → executor → preflight circular import cycle.\n   693→    from .preflight import execute_preflight_phases  # noqa: PLC0415\n   694→\n   695→    preflight_results = execute_preflight_phases(config)\n   696→\n   697→    try:\n   698→        for phase in config.active_phases:\n   699→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   700→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   701→                break\n   702→\n   703→            # Python-mode phases were already executed by preflight; skip here.\n   704→            if phase.execution_mode == \"python\":\n   705→                continue\n   706→\n   707→            # Skip-mode phases: record SKIPPED with no subprocess launched.\n   708→            if phase.execution_mode == \"skip\":\n   709→                _now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   710→                skip_result = PhaseResult(\n   711→                    phase=phase,\n   712→                    status=PhaseStatus.SKIPPED,\n   713→                    exit_code=0,\n   714→                    started_at=_now,\n   715→                    finished_at=_now,\n   716→                )\n   717→                sprint_result.phase_results.append(skip_result)\n   718→                logger.write_phase_result(skip_result)\n   719→                continue\n   720→\n   721→            # Per-phase isolation directory: exactly one file (the phase file)\n   722→            isolation_dir = config.results_dir / \".isolation\" / f\"phase-{phase.number}\"\n   723→            isolation_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   724→            shutil.copy2(phase.file, isolation_dir / phase.file.name)\n   725→\n   726→            try:\n   727→                # Reset monitor for this phase\n   728→                output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   729→                monitor.reset(output_path)\n   730→                monitor.start()\n   731→\n   732→                # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n   733→                if config.tmux_session_name:\n   734→                    update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n   735→\n   736→                # Launch claude with isolation env vars (CLAUDE_WORK_DIR → isolation_dir)\n   737→                _phase_env_vars = {\n   738→                    \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(isolation_dir),\n   739→                }\n   740→                proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase, env_vars=_phase_env_vars)\n   741→                proc_manager.start()\n   742→                started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   743→                # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n   744→                deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n   745→                logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n   746→\n   747→                debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n   748→\n   749→                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   750→\n   751→                # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n   752→                # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n   753→                _timed_out = False\n   754→                _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n   755→                _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n   756→                while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n   757→                    if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   758→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   759→                        break\n   760→                    if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   761→                        # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n   762→                        _timed_out = True\n   763→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   764→                        break\n   765→\n   766→                    ms = monitor.state\n   767→                    _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n   768→\n   769→                    debug_log(\n   770→                        _dbg,\n   771→                        \"poll_tick\",\n   772→                        phase=phase.number,\n   773→                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   774→                        poll_result=\"running\",\n   775→                        elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n   776→                        output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n   777→                        growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n   778→                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   779→                        stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n   780→                    )\n   781→\n   782→                    # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n   783→                    if (\n   784→                        config.stall_timeout > 0\n   785→                        and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   786→                        and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n   787→                        and not _stall_acted\n   788→                    ):\n   789→                        _stall_acted = True\n   790→                        debug_log(\n   791→                            _dbg,\n   792→                            \"watchdog_triggered\",\n   793→                            phase=phase.number,\n   794→                            action=config.stall_action,\n   795→                            stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   796→                            pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   797→                        )\n   798→                        if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   799→                            import sys\n   800→\n   801→                            print(\n   802→                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   803→                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n   804→                                file=sys.stderr,\n   805→                            )\n   806→                            _timed_out = True\n   807→                            proc_manager.terminate()\n   808→                            break\n   809→                        else:\n   810→                            # warn action: log and continue\n   811→                            import sys\n   812→\n   813→                            print(\n   814→                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   815→                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n   816→                                file=sys.stderr,\n   817→                            )\n   818→\n   819→                    # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n   820→                    if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n   821→                        _stall_acted = False\n   822→\n   823→                    # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n   824→                    # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n   825→                    try:\n   826→                        tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   827→                    except Exception as _tui_exc:\n   828→                        import sys\n   829→\n   830→                        print(\n   831→                            f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\",\n   832→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   833→                        )\n   834→                    time.sleep(0.5)\n   835→\n   836→                # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n   837→                # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n   838→                raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n   839→                if _timed_out:\n   840→                    exit_code = 124\n   841→                else:\n   842→                    exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n   843→                monitor.stop()\n   844→                finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   845→                _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   846→                debug_log(\n   847→                    _dbg,\n   848→                    \"PHASE_END\",\n   849→                    phase=phase.number,\n   850→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   851→                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   852→                )\n   853→\n   854→                # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n   855→                # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n   856→                # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n   857→                # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n   858→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   859→                    logger.write_phase_interrupt(\n   860→                        phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code\n   861→                    )\n   862→                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   863→                    break\n   864→\n   865→                # Write a preliminary result sentinel so _determine_phase_status()\n   866→                # always finds a result file for exit_code=0 phases that wrote no report.\n   867→                # Guard: only for successful exits; non-zero paths must not reach this.\n   868→                if exit_code == 0:\n   869→                    _wrote_preliminary = _write_preliminary_result(\n   870→                        config, phase, started_at.timestamp()\n   871→                    )\n   872→                    debug_log(\n   873→                        _dbg,\n   874→                        \"preliminary_result_write\",\n   875→                        path=f\"{'executor-preliminary (option_d)' if _wrote_preliminary else 'agent-written/option_a_or_noop'}\",\n   876→                    )\n   877→\n   878→                # Determine phase status\n   879→                status = _determine_phase_status(\n   880→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   881→                    result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n   882→                    output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   883→                    config=config,\n   884→                    phase=phase,\n   885→                    started_at=started_at.timestamp(),\n   886→                    error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   887→                )\n   888→\n   889→                # Write executor result file for downstream consumers.\n   890→                # Written AFTER status determination to avoid circularity.\n   891→                # Overwrites any agent-written file — executor is authoritative.\n   892→                _write_executor_result_file(\n   893→                    config=config,\n   894→                    phase=phase,\n   895→                    status=status,\n   896→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   897→                    monitor_state=monitor.state,\n   898→                    started_at=started_at,\n   899→                    finished_at=finished_at,\n   900→                )\n   901→\n   902→                # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n   903→                error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n   904→                error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n   905→\n   906→                phase_result = PhaseResult(\n   907→                    phase=phase,\n   908→                    status=status,\n   909→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   910→                    started_at=started_at,\n   911→                    finished_at=finished_at,\n   912→                    output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   913→                    error_bytes=error_bytes,\n   914→                    last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n   915→                    files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n   916→                )\n   917→                sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n   918→\n   919→                debug_log(\n   920→                    _dbg,\n   921→                    \"phase_complete\",\n   922→                    phase=phase.number,\n   923→                    status=status.value,\n   924→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   925→                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   926→                )\n   927→\n   928→                # Log and notify\n   929→                logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n   930→                notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n   931→\n   932→                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n   933→\n   934→                # Decide: continue or halt?\n   935→                if status.is_failure:\n   936→                    # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n   937→                    try:\n   938→                        collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n   939→                        bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n   940→                        classifier = FailureClassifier()\n   941→                        bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n   942→                        reporter = ReportGenerator()\n   943→                        diag_path = (\n   944→                            config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n   945→                        )\n   946→                        reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n   947→                        debug_log(\n   948→                            _dbg,\n   949→                            \"diagnostic_report\",\n   950→                            phase=phase.number,\n   951→                            category=bundle.category.value,\n   952→                            path=str(diag_path),\n   953→                        )\n   954→                    except Exception as _diag_exc:\n   955→                        debug_log(\n   956→                            _dbg,\n   957→                            \"diagnostic_error\",\n   958→                            phase=phase.number,\n   959→                            error=str(_diag_exc),\n   960→                        )\n   961→\n   962→                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n   963→                    sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n   964→                    break\n   965→\n   966→            finally:\n   967→                shutil.rmtree(isolation_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n   968→\n   969→        # Merge preflight results with main-loop results in original phase order.\n   970→        # Build a lookup from phase number → PhaseResult, main-loop results win\n   971→        # on conflict (they are the authoritative executor record for claude phases).\n   972→        _merged: dict[int, PhaseResult] = {r.phase.number: r for r in preflight_results}\n   973→        for r in sprint_result.phase_results:\n   974→            _merged[r.phase.number] = r\n   975→        sprint_result.phase_results = [\n   976→            _merged[p.number] for p in config.active_phases if p.number in _merged\n   977→        ]\n   978→\n   979→        # Sprint finished\n   980→        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   981→        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n   982→            # Verify all phases actually passed\n   983→            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n   984→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n   985→\n   986→        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n   987→        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n   988→        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n   989→\n   990→    finally:\n   991→        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n   992→        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n   993→        try:\n   994→            monitor.stop()\n   995→        except Exception:\n   996→            pass\n   997→        if proc_manager is not None:\n   998→            try:\n   999→                proc_manager.terminate()\n  1000→            except Exception:\n  1001→                pass\n  1002→        try:\n  1003→            tui.stop()\n  1004→        except Exception:\n  1005→            pass\n  1006→        try:\n  1007→            signal_handler.uninstall()\n  1008→        except Exception:\n  1009→            pass\n  1010→\n  1011→    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n  1012→    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n  1013→    try:\n  1014→        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n  1015→    except OSError:\n  1016→        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n  1017→\n  1018→    if _exitcode != 0:\n  1019→        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n  1020→\n  1021→\n  1022→def _classify_from_result_file(\n  1023→    result_file: Path,\n  1024→    started_at: float,\n  1025→) -> PhaseStatus | None:\n  1026→    \"\"\"Classify phase outcome from the agent-written result file.\n  1027→\n  1028→    Returns a PhaseStatus if the result file exists, is fresh (mtime > started_at),\n  1029→    and contains a recognizable EXIT_RECOMMENDATION. Returns None if the file is\n  1030→    missing, stale, or unreadable.\n  1031→    \"\"\"\n  1032→    if not result_file.exists():\n  1033→        return None\n  1034→    try:\n  1035→        mtime = result_file.stat().st_mtime\n  1036→    except OSError:\n  1037→        return None\n  1038→    if started_at > 0 and mtime < started_at:\n  1039→        # Stale file from a previous run — do not trust\n  1040→        return None\n  1041→    try:\n  1042→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n  1043→    except OSError:\n  1044→        return None\n  1045→    upper = content.upper()\n  1046→    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper:\n  1047→        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1048→    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper:\n  1049→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n  1050→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1051→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n  1052→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1053→        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1054→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1055→        return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n  1056→    return None\n  1057→\n  1058→\n  1059→def _check_checkpoint_pass(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> bool:\n  1060→    \"\"\"Return True if the end-of-phase checkpoint file exists with status PASS.\"\"\"\n  1061→    checkpoint_path = (\n  1062→        config.release_dir / \"checkpoints\" / f\"CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md\"\n  1063→    )\n  1064→    if not checkpoint_path.exists():\n  1065→        return False\n  1066→    try:\n  1067→        content = checkpoint_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\").upper()\n  1068→        return \"STATUS: PASS\" in content or \"**RESULT**: PASS\" in content\n  1069→    except OSError:\n  1070→        return False\n  1071→\n  1072→\n  1073→def _check_contamination(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n  1074→    \"\"\"Return list of artifact files containing cross-phase task ID patterns.\"\"\"\n  1075→    import re as _re\n  1076→\n  1077→    contaminated: list[str] = []\n  1078→    artifacts_dir = config.release_dir / \"artifacts\"\n  1079→    if not artifacts_dir.exists():\n  1080→        return contaminated\n  1081→    next_phase = phase.number + 1\n  1082→    pattern = _re.compile(rf\"T{next_phase:02d}\\.\\d{{2}}\", _re.IGNORECASE)\n  1083→    for md_file in artifacts_dir.rglob(\"*.md\"):\n  1084→        try:\n  1085→            if pattern.search(md_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")):\n  1086→                contaminated.append(str(md_file.relative_to(config.release_dir)))\n  1087→        except OSError:\n  1088→            pass\n  1089→    return contaminated\n  1090→\n  1091→\n  1092→def _write_crash_recovery_log(\n  1093→    config: SprintConfig,\n  1094→    phase: Phase,\n  1095→    contaminated: list[str],\n  1096→) -> None:\n  1097→    \"\"\"Append crash recovery entry to results/crash_recovery_log.md.\"\"\"\n  1098→    log_path = config.results_dir / \"crash_recovery_log.md\"\n  1099→    entry = (\n  1100→        f\"\\n## Phase {phase.number} — PASS_RECOVERED Recovery\\n\"\n  1101→        f\"**Timestamp**: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}\\n\"\n  1102→        f\"**Checkpoint**: checkpoints/CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md (PASS)\\n\"\n  1103→        f\"**Contamination check**: \"\n  1104→        + (\n  1105→            \"CLEAN\"\n  1106→            if not contaminated\n  1107→            else f\"WARNING — {len(contaminated)} file(s): {contaminated}\"\n  1108→        )\n  1109→        + \"\\n\"\n  1110→        \"**Action**: Phase reclassified ERROR→PASS_RECOVERED.\\n\"\n  1111→    )\n  1112→    try:\n  1113→        with open(log_path, \"a\") as f:\n  1114→            f.write(entry)\n  1115→    except OSError:\n  1116→        pass\n  1117→\n  1118→\n  1119→def _write_preliminary_result(\n  1120→    config: SprintConfig,\n  1121→    phase: Phase,\n  1122→    started_at: float,\n  1123→) -> bool:\n  1124→    \"\"\"Write a preliminary result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE sentinel.\n  1125→\n  1126→    This function is a deterministic fallback ensuring ``_determine_phase_status()``\n  1127→    always finds a valid result file for phases that exit 0 but write no report.\n  1128→    Without it, such phases return ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` because no result file is present.\n  1129→\n  1130→    **Ordering invariant**: Call *after* ``finished_at`` is captured and *before*\n  1131→    ``_determine_phase_status()`` is called. If called after status determination,\n  1132→    the sentinel file will not affect the already-computed status.\n  1133→\n  1134→    **Concurrency**: Assumes single-threaded execution (one phase at a time). If the\n  1135→    sprint loop is parallelised in future, this function must be replaced with an\n  1136→    ``O_EXCL``-based atomic write to prevent TOCTOU races between the exists-check\n  1137→    and the write.\n  1138→\n  1139→    **Sentinel contract**: Writes ``EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\\\n`` so that\n  1140→    ``_determine_phase_status()`` branch 3 (``result_file.exists()`` → CONTINUE check\n  1141→    at line 1024) returns ``PhaseStatus.PASS`` instead of falling through to the\n  1142→    ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` branch (line 1045). This sentinel is intentionally minimal;\n  1143→    the executor result file written by ``_write_executor_result_file()`` afterwards\n  1144→    will overwrite it with the authoritative structured report.\n  1145→\n  1146→    Args:\n  1147→        config: Sprint configuration providing ``result_file(phase)`` path.\n  1148→        phase: The phase whose result file to write.\n  1149→        started_at: Phase start time as a POSIX timestamp (``datetime.timestamp()``).\n  1150→            Used for the freshness guard: if a result file already exists with\n  1151→            ``st_mtime >= started_at`` and ``st_size > 0``, it is assumed to be a\n  1152→            valid agent-written file and is left untouched (no-op).\n  1153→\n  1154→    Returns:\n  1155→        ``True`` if the sentinel file was written, ``False`` if the file was preserved\n  1156→        (freshness guard triggered) or an ``OSError`` prevented the write. The boolean\n  1157→        is intended for telemetry logging only; callers should not branch on it.\n  1158→    \"\"\"\n  1159→    import logging as _logging\n  1160→\n  1161→    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n  1162→    # Freshness guard: if a valid, fresh agent-written file is present, do not overwrite.\n  1163→    if result_path.exists():\n  1164→        try:\n  1165→            st = result_path.stat()\n  1166→            if st.st_size > 0 and st.st_mtime >= started_at:\n  1167→                # Fresh, non-empty agent file — preserve it\n  1168→                return False\n  1169→        except OSError:\n  1170→            pass  # Treat stat failure as absent; fall through to write\n  1171→\n  1172→    # Write the sentinel: zero-byte, stale, or missing files are all overwritten.\n  1173→    try:\n  1174→        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1175→        result_path.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n  1176→        return True\n  1177→    except OSError as exc:\n  1178→        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(\n  1179→            \"preliminary result write failed: %s; phase may report PASS_NO_REPORT\",\n  1180→            exc,\n  1181→        )\n  1182→        return False\n  1183→\n  1184→\n  1185→def _write_executor_result_file(\n  1186→    config: SprintConfig,\n  1187→    phase: Phase,\n  1188→    status: PhaseStatus,\n  1189→    exit_code: int,\n  1190→    monitor_state: MonitorState,\n  1191→    started_at: datetime,\n  1192→    finished_at: datetime,\n  1193→) -> None:\n  1194→    \"\"\"Write executor-sourced result file for downstream consumers.\n  1195→\n  1196→    This is written AFTER _determine_phase_status returns, so it does not\n  1197→    create circularity. It provides a deterministic result file even when\n  1198→    the agent failed to write one.\n  1199→    \"\"\"\n  1200→    duration = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n  1201→    recommendation = \"CONTINUE\" if status.is_success else \"HALT\"\n  1202→    content = (\n  1203→        \"---\\n\"\n  1204→        f\"phase: {phase.number}\\n\"\n  1205→        f\"status: {'PASS' if status.is_success else 'FAIL'}\\n\"\n  1206→        f\"tasks_total: 1\\n\"\n  1207→        f\"tasks_passed: {1 if status.is_success else 0}\\n\"\n  1208→        f\"tasks_failed: {0 if status.is_success else 1}\\n\"\n  1209→        \"---\\n\"\n  1210→        \"\\n\"\n  1211→        f\"# Phase {phase.number} — Executor Result Report\\n\"\n  1212→        \"\\n\"\n  1213→        f\"| Phase | Status | Exit Code | Duration |\\n\"\n  1214→        f\"|-------|--------|-----------|----------|\\n\"\n  1215→        f\"| {phase.number} | {status.value} | {exit_code} | {duration:.1f}s |\\n\"\n  1216→        \"\\n\"\n  1217→        f\"**Source**: executor (not agent self-report)\\n\"\n  1218→        f\"**Output bytes**: {monitor_state.output_bytes}\\n\"\n  1219→        f\"**Last task ID**: {monitor_state.last_task_id or 'n/a'}\\n\"\n  1220→        f\"**Files changed**: {monitor_state.files_changed}\\n\"\n  1221→        \"\\n\"\n  1222→        f\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: {recommendation}\\n\"\n  1223→    )\n  1224→    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n  1225→    try:\n  1226→        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1227→        result_path.write_text(content)\n  1228→    except OSError:\n  1229→        pass  # Non-fatal — best effort\n  1230→\n  1231→\n  1232→def _determine_phase_status(\n  1233→    exit_code: int,\n  1234→    result_file: Path,\n  1235→    output_file: Path,\n  1236→    *,\n  1237→    config: SprintConfig | None = None,\n  1238→    phase: Phase | None = None,\n  1239→    started_at: float = 0.0,\n  1240→    error_file: Path | None = None,\n  1241→) -> PhaseStatus:\n  1242→    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n  1243→\n  1244→    Priority:\n  1245→    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n  1246→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n  1247→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n  1248→    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n  1249→    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n  1250→    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n  1251→    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n  1252→    \"\"\"\n  1253→    if exit_code == 124:\n  1254→        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n  1255→    if exit_code != 0:\n  1256→        # Path 1 — Specific: context exhaustion (Spec B S2)\n  1257→        # detect_prompt_too_long reads NDJSON output for \"Prompt is too long\"\n  1258→        if detect_prompt_too_long(output_file, error_path=error_file):\n  1259→            # Check if the agent managed to write a result file before exhaustion\n  1260→            result_status = _classify_from_result_file(result_file, started_at)\n  1261→            if result_status is not None:\n  1262→                return result_status\n  1263→            # No valid result file — context exhausted without completing\n  1264→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n  1265→\n  1266→        # Path 2 — General: checkpoint inference (Spec A SOL-C)\n  1267→        # Reads agent-written checkpoint files (pre-crash evidence)\n  1268→        if config is not None and phase is not None:\n  1269→            if _check_checkpoint_pass(config, phase):\n  1270→                contaminated = _check_contamination(config, phase)\n  1271→                _write_crash_recovery_log(config, phase, contaminated)\n  1272→                if not contaminated:\n  1273→                    return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n  1274→\n  1275→        # Path 3 — Default: unchanged\n  1276→        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n  1277→\n  1278→    if result_file.exists():\n  1279→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n  1280→        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n  1281→        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n  1282→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n  1283→        upper = content.upper()\n  1284→        # Sentinel contract: EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE is written by\n  1285→        # _write_preliminary_result() as a deterministic fallback for phases that\n  1286→        # exit 0 but produce no agent result file. The presence of this sentinel\n  1287→        # here causes the phase to return PASS rather than fall through to\n  1288→        # PASS_NO_REPORT (branch 4). _write_executor_result_file() overwrites this\n  1289→        # file after status determination with the authoritative structured report.\n  1290→        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n  1291→        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n  1292→        if has_halt:\n  1293→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1294→        if has_continue:\n  1295→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n  1296→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1297→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n  1298→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1299→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1300→        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n  1301→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1302→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1303→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n  1304→\n  1305→    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n  1306→        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n  1307→        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n  1308→        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n  1309→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n  1310→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n  1311→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n  1312→\n  1313→    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n  1314→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e4b79c92-efe6-439a-8057-fa8d1eeb7b4c","uuid":"684b18ec-4ee4-43fe-9913-f0a89ded61e4","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:12:55.179Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py","content":"\"\"\"Sprint ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n\n    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n\n    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    scoped_work_dir: Path\n    git_boundary: Path\n    plugin_dir: Path\n    settings_dir: Path\n\n    @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n\n    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n            return \"FAIL\"\n        return \"PARTIAL\"\n\n    def to_yaml(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a YAML string.\n\n        Produces a machine-readable YAML document with standardized fields\n        for downstream tooling and TUI display.\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n            f\"tasks_incomplete: {self.tasks_incomplete}\",\n            f\"tasks_not_attempted: {self.tasks_not_attempted}\",\n            f\"budget_remaining: {self.budget_remaining}\",\n            f\"total_turns_consumed: {self.total_turns_consumed}\",\n            f\"total_duration_seconds: {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}\",\n            \"tasks:\",\n        ]\n        for tr in self.task_results:\n            lines.append(f\"  - task_id: {tr.task.task_id}\")\n            lines.append(f'    title: \"{tr.task.title}\"')\n            lines.append(f\"    status: {tr.status.value}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    gate_outcome: {tr.gate_outcome.value}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    turns_consumed: {tr.turns_consumed}\")\n            lines.append(f\" lines.append(f\"  - {tid}\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:
