task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n    budget_remaining: int = 0,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n        budget_remaining=budget_remaining,\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS)\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL)\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(r.duration_seconds for r in task_results)\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef run_wiring_safeguard_checks(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    report: object | None = None,\n) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Pre-activation safeguards for wiring analysis (SC-010, section 7 Phase 1).\n\n    Checks run before the first wiring analysis in a sprint session:\n    1. Zero-match warning: >50 files scanned but 0 findings → suspicious\n    2. Whitelist validation: attempt to load whitelist, warn if parse fails\n    3. provider_dir_names check: verify configured directories exist\n\n    Safeguards produce warnings only; they never block sprint execution.\n\n    Returns a list of warning messages (empty if all checks pass).\n    \"\"\"\n    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig, load_whitelist\n\n    warnings: list[str] = []\n\n    # Check 1: Zero-match warning (requires a report from a prior run)\n    if report is not None:\n        files_scanned = getattr(report, \"files_analyzed\", 0)\n        total_findings = getattr(report, \"total_findings\", 0)\n        if files_scanned > 50 and total_findings == 0:\n            msg = (\n                f\"Zero-match warning: {files_scanned} files scanned but \"\n                f\"0 findings produced — verify wiring analysis configuration\"\n            )\n            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n            warnings.append(msg)\n\n    # Check 2: Whitelist validation\n    # Try to parse with \"soft\" mode so malformed YAML raises instead of silently\n    # returning empty. This is a safeguard check, not runtime behavior.\n    whitelist_path = config.release_dir / \"src\" / \"superclaude\" / \"cli\" / \"audit\" / \"wiring_whitelist.yaml\"\n    if whitelist_path.exists():\n        try:\n            import yaml as _yaml\n            raw = _yaml.safe_load(whitelist_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n            if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n                msg = f\"Whitelist validation failed: file at {whitelist_path} is not a YAML mapping\"\n                _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n                warnings.append(msg)\n        except Exception as exc:\n            msg = f\"Whitelist validation failed: {exc}\"\n            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n            warnings.append(msg)\n\n    # Check 3: provider_dir_names sanity check\n    wiring_config = WiringConfig()\n    source_dir = config.release_dir\n    for dir_name in wiring_config.provider_dir_names:\n        found = list(source_dir.rglob(dir_name))\n        dirs_found = [d for d in found if d.is_dir()]\n        if not dirs_found:\n            msg = (\n                f\"Provider directory '{dir_name}' not found under \"\n                f\"{source_dir} — orphan module detection may miss files\"\n            )\n            _wiring_logger.warning(msg)\n            warnings.append(msg)\n\n    return warnings\n\n\ndef run_post_task_wiring_hook(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    task_result: TaskResult,\n) -> TaskResult:\n    \"\"\"Run post-task wiring analysis based on config.wiring_gate_mode.\n\n    Mode behavior:\n    - off: skip analysis entirely, return task_result unchanged\n    - shadow: run analysis, log findings, task status unchanged (SC-006)\n    - soft: run analysis, warn on critical findings, task status unchanged\n    - full: run analysis, block (set FAIL) on critical+major findings\n\n    Returns the (possibly modified) TaskResult. Only full mode may change\n    task status to FAIL when blocking findings exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    mode = config.wiring_gate_mode\n\n    if mode == \"off\":\n        return task_result\n\n    # Lazy import to avoid circular dependency at module level\n    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_gate import run_wiring_analysis, WiringReport\n    from superclaude.cli.audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig\n\n    source_dir = config.release_dir\n    wiring_config = WiringConfig(rollout_mode=\"shadow\" if mode == \"off\" else mode if mode in (\"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\") else \"shadow\")\n\n    try:\n        report = run_wiring_analysis(wiring_config, source_dir)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        _wiring_logger.warning(\n            \"Wiring analysis failed for task %s: %s — continuing\",\n            task.task_id,\n            exc,\n        )\n        return task_result\n\n    total = report.total_findings\n    blocking = report.blocking_count(mode)\n\n    _wiring_logger.info(\n        \"Wiring hook [%s] task=%s: %d findings, %d blocking (%.4fs)\",\n        mode,\n        task.task_id,\n        total,\n        blocking,\n        report.scan_duration_seconds,\n    )\n\n    if mode == \"shadow\":\n        # SC-006: log findings without affecting task status\n        if total > 0:\n            _wiring_logger.info(\n                \"Shadow mode: %d findings logged for task %s (status unchanged)\",\n                total,\n                task.task_id,\n            )\n        return task_result\n\n    if mode == \"soft\":\n        # Warn on critical findings but don't change status\n        critical_count = sum(\n            1 for f in report.unsuppressed_findings if f.severity == \"critical\"\n        )\n        if critical_count > 0:\n            _wiring_logger.warning(\n                \"Soft mode: %d critical findings for task %s\",\n                critical_count,\n                task.task_id,\n            )\n        return task_result\n\n    if mode == \"full\":\n        # Block on critical+major findings\n        if blocking > 0:\n            _wiring_logger.error(\n                \"Full mode: %d blocking findings for task %s — marking FAIL\",\n                blocking,\n                task.task_id,\n            )\n            task_result.status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n            task_result.gate_outcome = GateOutcome.FAIL\n        return task_result\n\n    return task_result\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        result = TaskResult(\n            task=task,\n            status=status,\n            turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n            exit_code=exit_code,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n            output_bytes=output_bytes,\n        )\n\n        # Post-task wiring hook: run wiring analysis per config.wiring_gate_mode\n        result = run_post_task_wiring_hook(task, config, result)\n\n        results.append(result)\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    # Startup orphan cleanup: remove stale isolation dirs from crashed previous runs\n    shutil.rmtree(config.results_dir / \".isolation\", ignore_errors=True)\n\n    # Execute all python-mode phases via preflight executor before the main loop.\n    # Removing this single call reverts to all-Claude behavior (R-051 rollback property).\n    # Lazy import breaks the preflight → executor → preflight circular import cycle.\n    from .preflight import execute_preflight_phases  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n    preflight_results = execute_preflight_phases(config)\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Python-mode phases were already executed by preflight; skip here.\n            if phase.execution_mode == \"python\":\n                continue\n\n            # Skip-mode phases: record SKIPPED with no subprocess launched.\n            if phase.execution_mode == \"skip\":\n                _now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                skip_result = PhaseResult(\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=PhaseStatus.SKIPPED,\n                    exit_code=0,\n                    started_at=_now,\n                    finished_at=_now,\n                )\n                sprint_result.phase_results.append(skip_result)\n                logger.write_phase_result(skip_result)\n                continue\n\n            # Per-phase isolation directory: exactly one file (the phase file)\n            isolation_dir = config.results_dir / \".isolation\" / f\"phase-{phase.number}\"\n            isolation_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            shutil.copy2(phase.file, isolation_dir / phase.file.name)\n\n            try:\n                # Reset monitor for this phase\n                output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n                monitor.reset(output_path)\n                monitor.start()\n\n                # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n                if config.tmux_session_name:\n                    update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n                # Launch claude with isolation env vars (CLAUDE_WORK_DIR → isolation_dir)\n                _phase_env_vars = {\n                    \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(isolation_dir),\n                }\n                proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase, env_vars=_phase_env_vars)\n                proc_manager.start()\n                started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n                deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n                logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n                debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n                # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n                # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n                _timed_out = False\n                _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n                _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n                while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                    if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                        # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n\n                    ms = monitor.state\n                    _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"poll_tick\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                        poll_result=\"running\",\n                        elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                        output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                        growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                    )\n\n                    # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                    if (\n                        config.stall_timeout > 0\n                        and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                        and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                        and not _stall_acted\n                    ):\n                        _stall_acted = True\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            action=config.stall_action,\n                            stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                            pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                        )\n                        if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                            import sys\n\n                            print(\n                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                                file=sys.stderr,\n                            )\n                            _timed_out = True\n                            proc_manager.terminate()\n                            break\n                        else:\n                            # warn action: log and continue\n                            import sys\n\n                            print(\n                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                                file=sys.stderr,\n                            )\n\n                    # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                    if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                        _stall_acted = False\n\n                    # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                    # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                    try:\n                        tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                    except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                        import sys\n\n                        print(\n                            f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                    time.sleep(0.5)\n\n                # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n                # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n                raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n                if _timed_out:\n                    exit_code = 124\n                else:\n                    exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n                monitor.stop()\n                finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"PHASE_END\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n                )\n\n                # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n                # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n                # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n                # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    logger.write_phase_interrupt(\n                        phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code\n                    )\n                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                    break\n\n                # Write a preliminary result sentinel so _determine_phase_status()\n                # always finds a result file for exit_code=0 phases that wrote no report.\n                # Guard: only for successful exits; non-zero paths must not reach this.\n                if exit_code == 0:\n                    _wrote_preliminary = _write_preliminary_result(\n                        config, phase, started_at.timestamp()\n                    )\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"preliminary_result_write\",\n                        path=f\"{'executor-preliminary (option_d)' if _wrote_preliminary else 'agent-written/option_a_or_noop'}\",\n                    )\n\n                # Determine phase status\n                status = _determine_phase_status(\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                    output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n                    config=config,\n                    phase=phase,\n                    started_at=started_at.timestamp(),\n                    error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n                )\n\n                # Write executor result file for downstream consumers.\n                # Written AFTER status determination to avoid circularity.\n                # Overwrites any agent-written file — executor is authoritative.\n                _write_executor_result_file(\n                    config=config,\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=status,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    monitor_state=monitor.state,\n                    started_at=started_at,\n                    finished_at=finished_at,\n                )\n\n                # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n                error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n                error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n                phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=status,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    started_at=started_at,\n                    finished_at=finished_at,\n                    output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                    error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                    last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                    files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n                )\n                sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"phase_complete\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    status=status.value,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n                )\n\n                # Log and notify\n                logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n                notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n                # Decide: continue or halt?\n                if status.is_failure:\n                    # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                    try:\n                        collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                        bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                        classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                        bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                        reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                        diag_path = (\n                            config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                        )\n                        reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"diagnostic_report\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            category=bundle.category.value,\n                            path=str(diag_path),\n                        )\n                    except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"diagnostic_error\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            error=str(_diag_exc),\n                        )\n\n                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                    sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                    break\n\n            finally:\n                shutil.rmtree(isolation_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n\n        # Merge preflight results with main-loop results in original phase order.\n        # Build a lookup from phase number → PhaseResult, main-loop results win\n        # on conflict (they are the authoritative executor record for claude phases).\n        _merged: dict[int, PhaseResult] = {r.phase.number: r for r in preflight_results}\n        for r in sprint_result.phase_results:\n            _merged[r.phase.number] = r\n        sprint_result.phase_results = [\n            _merged[p.number] for p in config.active_phases if p.number in _merged\n        ]\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _classify_from_result_file(\n    result_file: Path,\n    started_at: float,\n) -> PhaseStatus | None:\n    \"\"\"Classify phase outcome from the agent-written result file.\n\n    Returns a PhaseStatus if the result file exists, is fresh (mtime > started_at),\n    and contains a recognizable EXIT_RECOMMENDATION. Returns None if the file is\n    missing, stale, or unreadable.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not result_file.exists():\n        return None\n    try:\n        mtime = result_file.stat().st_mtime\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    if started_at > 0 and mtime < started_at:\n        # Stale file from a previous run — do not trust\n        return None\n    try:\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    upper = content.upper()\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper:\n        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper:\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    return None\n\n\ndef _check_checkpoint_pass(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if the end-of-phase checkpoint file exists with status PASS.\"\"\"\n    checkpoint_path = (\n        config.release_dir / \"checkpoints\" / f\"CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md\"\n    )\n    if not checkpoint_path.exists():\n        return False\n    try:\n        content = checkpoint_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\").upper()\n        return \"STATUS: PASS\" in content or \"**RESULT**: PASS\" in content\n    except OSError:\n        return False\n\n\ndef _check_contamination(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Return list of artifact files containing cross-phase task ID patterns.\"\"\"\n    import re as _re\n\n    contaminated: list[str] = []\n    artifacts_dir = config.release_dir / \"artifacts\"\n    if not artifacts_dir.exists():\n        return contaminated\n    next_phase = phase.number + 1\n    pattern = _re.compile(rf\"T{next_phase:02d}\\.\\d{{2}}\", _re.IGNORECASE)\n    for md_file in artifacts_dir.rglob(\"*.md\"):\n        try:\n            if pattern.search(md_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")):\n                contaminated.append(str(md_file.relative_to(config.release_dir)))\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n    return contaminated\n\n\ndef _write_crash_recovery_log(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    contaminated: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Append crash recovery entry to results/crash_recovery_log.md.\"\"\"\n    log_path = config.results_dir / \"crash_recovery_log.md\"\n    entry = (\n        f\"\\n## Phase {phase.number} — PASS_RECOVERED Recovery\\n\"\n        f\"**Timestamp**: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}\\n\"\n        f\"**Checkpoint**: checkpoints/CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md (PASS)\\n\"\n        f\"**Contamination check**: \"\n        + (\n            \"CLEAN\"\n            if not contaminated\n            else f\"WARNING — {len(contaminated)} file(s): {contaminated}\"\n        )\n        + \"\\n\"\n        \"**Action**: Phase reclassified ERROR→PASS_RECOVERED.\\n\"\n    )\n    try:\n        with open(log_path, \"a\") as f:\n            f.write(entry)\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef _write_preliminary_result(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    started_at: float,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Write a preliminary result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE sentinel.\n\n    This function is a deterministic fallback ensuring ``_determine_phase_status()``\n    always finds a valid result file for phases that exit 0 but write no report.\n    Without it, such phases return ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` because no result file is present.\n\n    **Ordering invariant**: Call *after* ``finished_at`` is captured and *before*\n    ``_determine_phase_status()`` is called. If called after status determination,\n    the sentinel file will not affect the already-computed status.\n\n    **Concurrency**: Assumes single-threaded execution (one phase at a time). If the\n    sprint loop is parallelised in future, this function must be replaced with an\n    ``O_EXCL``-based atomic write to prevent TOCTOU races between the exists-check\n    and the write.\n\n    **Sentinel contract**: Writes ``EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\\\n`` so that\n    ``_determine_phase_status()`` branch 3 (``result_file.exists()`` → CONTINUE check\n    at line 1024) returns ``PhaseStatus.PASS`` instead of falling through to the\n    ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` branch (line 1045). This sentinel is intentionally minimal;\n    the executor result file written by ``_write_executor_result_file()`` afterwards\n    will overwrite it with the authoritative structured report.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing ``result_file(phase)`` path.\n        phase: The phase whose result file to write.\n        started_at: Phase start time as a POSIX timestamp (``datetime.timestamp()``).\n            Used for the freshness guard: if a result file already exists with\n            ``st_mtime >= started_at`` and ``st_size > 0``, it is assumed to be a\n            valid agent-written file and is left untouched (no-op).\n\n    Returns:\n        ``True`` if the sentinel file was written, ``False`` if the file was preserved\n        (freshness guard triggered) or an ``OSError`` prevented the write. The boolean\n        is intended for telemetry logging only; callers should not branch on it.\n    \"\"\"\n    import logging as _logging\n\n    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n    # Freshness guard: if a valid, fresh agent-written file is present, do not overwrite.\n    if result_path.exists():\n        try:\n            st = result_path.stat()\n            if st.st_size > 0 and st.st_mtime >= started_at:\n                # Fresh, non-empty agent file — preserve it\n                return False\n        except OSError:\n            pass  # Treat stat failure as absent; fall through to write\n\n    # Write the sentinel: zero-byte, stale, or missing files are all overwritten.\n    try:\n        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        result_path.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        return True\n    except OSError as exc:\n        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(\n            \"preliminary result write failed: %s; phase may report PASS_NO_REPORT\",\n            exc,\n        )\n        return False\n\n\ndef _write_executor_result_file(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    status: PhaseStatus,\n    exit_code: int,\n    monitor_state: MonitorState,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write executor-sourced result file for downstream consumers.\n\n    This is written AFTER _determine_phase_status returns, so it does not\n    create circularity. It provides a deterministic result file even when\n    the agent failed to write one.\n    \"\"\"\n    duration = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    recommendation = \"CONTINUE\" if status.is_success else \"HALT\"\n    content = (\n        \"---\\n\"\n        f\"phase: {phase.number}\\n\"\n        f\"status: {'PASS' if status.is_success else 'FAIL'}\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_total: 1\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_passed: {1 if status.is_success else 0}\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_failed: {0 if status.is_success else 1}\\n\"\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"# Phase {phase.number} — Executor Result Report\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"| Phase | Status | Exit Code | Duration |\\n\"\n        f\"|-------|--------|-----------|----------|\\n\"\n        f\"| {phase.number} | {status.value} | {exit_code} | {duration:.1f}s |\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"**Source**: executor (not agent self-report)\\n\"\n        f\"**Output bytes**: {monitor_state.output_bytes}\\n\"\n        f\"**Last task ID**: {monitor_state.last_task_id or 'n/a'}\\n\"\n        f\"**Files changed**: {monitor_state.files_changed}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: {recommendation}\\n\"\n    )\n    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n    try:\n        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        result_path.write_text(content)\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # Non-fatal — best effort\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n    *,\n    config: SprintConfig | None = None,\n    phase: Phase | None = None,\n    started_at: float = 0.0,\n    error_file: Path | None = None,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        # Path 1 — Specific: context exhaustion (Spec B S2)\n        # detect_prompt_too_long reads NDJSON output for \"Prompt is too long\"\n        if detect_prompt_too_long(output_file, error_path=error_file):\n            # Check if the agent managed to write a result file before exhaustion\n            result_status = _classify_from_result_file(result_file, started_at)\n            if result_status is not None:\n                return result_status\n            # No valid result file — context exhausted without completing\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n        # Path 2 — General: checkpoint inference (Spec A SOL-C)\n        # Reads agent-written checkpoint files (pre-crash evidence)\n        if config is not None and phase is not None:\n            if _check_checkpoint_pass(config, phase):\n                contaminated = _check_contamination(config, phase)\n                _write_crash_recovery_log(config, phase, contaminated)\n                if not contaminated:\n                    return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n\n        # Path 3 — Default: unchanged\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        # Sentinel contract: EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE is written by\n        # _write_preliminary_result() as a deterministic fallback for phases that\n        # exit 0 but produce no agent result file. The presence of this sentinel\n        # here causes the phase to return PASS rather than fall through to\n        # PASS_NO_REPORT (branch 4). _write_executor_result_file() overwrites this\n        # file after status determination with the authoritative structured report.\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","numLines":1314,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1314}}}

   assert ledger.can_launch() is False\n   554→        assert ledger.can_remediate() is False\n   555→\n   556→    def test_debit_zero_is_noop(self):\n   557→        \"\"\"debit(0) is valid and does     ledger.credit(0)\n   567→        assert ledger.reimbursed == 0\n   568→        assert ledger.available() == 50\n   569→\n   570→    def test_credit_exceeds_consumed_increases_available(self):\n      ledger.credit(20)\n   575→        assert ledger.available() == 60  # 50 - 10 + 20\n   576→\n   577→    def test_budget_monotonicity_consumed_never_decreases(self):\n   578→        \"\"\"consumed only increases — there is no method to decrease it.\"\"\"\n   579→        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n   580→        ledger.debit(10)\n   581→        ledger.debit(5)\n   582→        ledger.credit(20)  # credit doesn't decrease consumed\n   583→        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # still 10 + 5\n   584→\n   585→    def test_budget_monotonicity_across_many_operations(self):\n   586→        \"\"\"consumed never decreases across 15+ mixed operations.\"\"\"\n   587→        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200)\n   588→        prev_consumed = 0\n   589→        ops = [\n   590→            (\"debit\", 10),\n   591→            (\"debit\", 5),\n   592→            (\"credit\", 3),\n   593→            (\"debit\", 20),\n   594→            (\"credit\", 10),\n   595→            (\"debit\", 1),\n   596→            (\"credit\", 0),\n   597→            (\"debit\", 15),\n   598→            (\"debit\", 0),\n   599→            (\"credit\", 5),\n   600→            (\"debit\", 8),\n   601→            (\"credit\", 2),\n   602→            (\"debit\", 12),\n   603→            (\"debit\", 3),\n   604→            (\"credit\", 7),\n   605→        ]\n   606→        for op, amount in ops:\n   607→            if op == \"debit\":\n   608→                ledger.debit(amount)\n   609→            else:\n   610→                ledger.credit(amount)\n   611→            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n   612→                f\"consumed decreased from {prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} \"\n   613→                f\"after {op}({amount})\"\n   614→            )\n   615→            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n   616→\n   617→        # Verify final arithmetic\n   618→        total_debits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"debit\")\n   619→        total_credits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"credit\")\n   620→        assert ledger.consumed == total_debits\n   621→        assert ledger.reimbursed == total_credits\n   622→        assert ledger.available() == 200 - total_debits + total_credits\n   623→\n   624→    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_launch(self):\n   625→        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_allocation allows rate=0.8: net cost per passing task = 4 turns (spec §4.1).\"\"\"\n   636→        import math\n   637→\n   638→        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8)\n   639→        # Simulate one task: 8 turns consumed, then credit floor(8 * 0.8) = 6\n   640→        ledger.debit(8)\n   641→        reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n   642→        assert reimbursed == 6\n   643→        ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n   644→        # Also add 2 overhead turns\n   645→        ledger.debit(2)\n   646→        # Net cost = 8 + 2 - 6 = 4\n   647→        assert ledger.consumed == 10  # 8 + 2\n   648→        assert ledger.reimbursed == 6\n   649→        assert ledger.available() == 200 - 10 + 6  # = 196\n   650→\n   651→    def test_max_sustainable_tasks_at_08(self):\n   652→        \"\"\"Verify sustainability boundary: budget=200, rate=0.8, net_cost=4/task → ~50 tasks (spec §4.2).\"\"\"\n   653→        import math\n   654→\n   655→        ledger = TurnLedger(\n   656→            initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8, minimum_allocation=8\n   657→        )\n   658→        task_count = 0\n   659→        while ledger.can_launch():\n   660→            ledger.debit(8)\n   661→            reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n   662→            ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n   663→            ledger.debit(2)  # overhead\n   664→            task_count += 1\n   665→            if task_count > 100:  # safety bound\n   666→                break\n   667→        # 200 / 4 net cost = 50 total capacity, but can_launch requires minimum_allocation=8\n   668→        # After 49 tasks: available = 200 - 49*10 + 49*6 = 4 < 8, so loop exits at 49\n   669→        assert task_count == 49\n   670→        assert ledger.available() == 4\n   671→\n   672→    def test_46_task_sprint_sustainability(self):\n   673→        \"\"\"NFR-003: a 46-task sprint at rate=0.8, budget=200 completes with 16-turn margin.\"\"\"\n   674→        import math\n   675→\n   676→        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8)\n   677→        for _ in range(46):\n   678→            ledger.debit(8)\n   679→            reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n   680→            ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n   681→            ledger.debit(2)  # overhead\n   682→        assert ledger.available() > 0\n   683→        # Expected: 200 - 46*(8+2) + 46*6 = 200 - 460 + 276 = 16\n   684→        assert ledger.available() == 16\n   685→\n   686→    def test_budget_exhaustion_property(self):\n   687→        \"\"\"NFR-008: for rate < 1.0, budget monotonically decays and reaches 0 in finite steps.\"\"\"\n   688→        import math\n   689→        import random\n   690→\n   691→        rng = random.Random(42)  # deterministic seed per R-005\n   692→        for _ in range(20):  # 20 trials\n   693→            budget = rng.randint(50, 500)\n   694→            rate = round(rng.uniform(0.1, 0.99), 2)\n   695→            turns_per_task = rng.randint(1, 50)\n   696→            ledger = TurnLedger(\n   697→                initial_budget=budget,\n   698→                reimbursement_rate=rate,\n   699→                minimum_allocation=turns_per_task,\n   700→            )\n   701→\n   702→            prev_available = ledger.available()\n   703→            steps = 0\n   704→            while ledger.can_launch() and steps < 10000:\n   705→                ledger.debit(turns_per_task)\n   706→                reimbursed = math.floor(turns_per_task * rate)\n   707→                ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n   708→                # Verify monotonic decay: each cycle net cost > 0\n   709→                assert ledger.available() < prev_available, (\n   710→                    f\"Budget did not decrease: {prev_available} -> {ledger.available()} \"\n   711→                    f\"(rate={rate}, turns={turns_per_task})\"\n   712→                )\n   713→                prev_available = ledger.available()\n   714→                steps += 1\n   715→            # Budget must have been exhausted in finite steps\n   716→            assert not ledger.can_launch(), (\n   717→                f\"Budget never exhausted after {steps} steps\"\n   718→            )\n   719→\n   720→    def test_rate_boundary_validation(self):\n   721→        \"\"\"SC-001: verify TurnLedger behavior at rate boundary values.\"\"\"\n   722→        import math\n   723→\n   724→        # rate=0.0 → zero reimbursement, valid\n   725→        ledger_zero = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=0.0)\n   726→        ledger_zero.debit(10)\n   727→        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_zero.reimbursement_rate)\n   728→        ledger_zero.credit(reimbursed)\n   729→        assert reimbursed == 0\n   730→        assert ledger_zero.available() == 90  # no reimbursement\n   731→\n   732→        # rate=0.99 → high reimbursement, valid\n   733→        ledger_high = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=0.99)\n   734→        ledger_high.debit(10)\n   735→        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_high.reimbursement_rate)\n   736→        ledger_high.credit(reimbursed)\n   737→        assert reimbursed == 9\n   738→        assert ledger_high.available() == 99\n   739→\n   740→        # rate=1.0 → no decay, net cost = 0 per task (infinite budget)\n   741→        # SC-001 says this should be rejected, but TurnLedger currently has no validation.\n   742→        # This test documents the current behavior: rate=1.0 is accepted but causes zero net cost.\n   743→        ledger_one = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=1.0)\n   744→        ledger_one.debit(10)\n   745→        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_one.reimbursement_rate)\n   746→        ledger_one.credit(reimbursed)\n   747→        assert reimbursed == 10\n   748→        assert ledger_one.available() == 100  # no decay — budget never depletes\n   749→\n   750→        # rate=-0.1 → negative reimbursement (penalizes)\n   751→        # SC-001 says this should be rejected, but TurnLedger currently has no validation.\n   752→        # This test documents the current behavior: negative rate produces negative credits.\n   753→        ledger_neg = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=-0.1)\n   754→        ledger_neg.debit(10)\n   755→        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_neg.reimbursement_rate)\n   756→        # floor(10 * -0.1) = floor(-1.0) = -1\n   757→        assert reimbursed == -1\n   758→        # credit(-1) should raise ValueError per existing validation\n   759→        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"non-negative\"):\n   760→            ledger_neg.credit(reimbursed)\n   761→\n   762→\n   763→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   764→# GateOutcome ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestTurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Tests for TurnLedger budget arithmetic.\"\"\"\n\n    def ledger.available() == 35\n\n    def test_debit_negative_raises(self):\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"non-negative\"):\n == 5\n        assert ledger.available() == 35  # 50 - 20 + 5\n\n    def test_credit_negative_raises(self):\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"non-negative\"):\n            ledger.credit(-1)\n\n    def test_available_formula(self):\n        \"\"\"available() = initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100)\n        ledger.debit(30)\n        ledger.credit(10)\n        assert ledger.available() == 100 - 30 + 10\n\n    def test_can_launch_true(self):\n        ledger =        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_remediation_budget=3)\n        ledger.debit(48)\n        assert ledger.available() == 2\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is False\n\n    def test_can_remediate_exact_boundary(self):\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_remediation_budget=3)\n        ledger.debit(47)\n        assert ledger.available() == 3\n        assert  \"\"\"With zero budget, can_launch() returns False.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=0, minimum_allocation=5)\n        assert ledger.can_launch() is False\n\n    def test_zero_budget_can_remediate_false(self):\n        \"\"\"With zero budget, can_remediate() returns False.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=0, minimum_remediation_budget=3)\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is False\n\n    def test_over_budget_debit_makes_available_negative(self):\n        \"\"\"Debiting more than available results in negative available.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=10)\n        ledger.debit(15)\n        assert ledger.available() == -5\n        assert ledger.can_launch() is False\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is False\n\n    def test_debit_zero_is_noop(self):\n        \"\"\"debit(0) is valid and does not change state.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(0)\n        assert ledger.consumed == 0\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n    def test_credit_zero_is_noop(self):\n        \"\"\"credit(0) is valid and does not change state.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.credit(0)\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == 0\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n    def test_credit_exceeds_consumed_increases_available(self):\n        \"\"\"Crediting more than consumed is allowed (increases available beyond initial).\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.credit(20)\n        assert ledger.available() == 60  # 50 - 10 + 20\n\n    def test_budget_monotonicity_consumed_never_decreases(self):\n        \"\"\"consumed only increases — there is no method to decrease it.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.debit(5)\n        ledger.credit(20)  # credit doesn't decrease consumed\n        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # still 10 + 5\n\n    def test_budget_monotonicity_across_many_operations(self):\n        \"\"\"consumed never decreases across 15+ mixed operations.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200)\n        prev_consumed = 0\n        ops = [\n            (\"debit\", 10),\n            (\"debit\", 5),\n            (\"credit\", 3),\n            (\"debit\", 20),\n            (\"credit\", 10),\n            (\"debit\", 1),\n            (\"credit\", 0),\n            (\"debit\", 15),\n            (\"debit\", 0),\n            (\"credit\", 5),\n            (\"debit\", 8),\n            (\"credit\", 2),\n            (\"debit\", 12),\n            (\"debit\", 3),\n            (\"credit\", 7),\n        ]\n        for op, amount in ops:\n            if op == \"debit\":\n                ledger.debit(amount)\n            else:\n                ledger.credit(amount)\n            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n                f\"consumed decreased from {prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} \"\n                f\"after {op}({amount})\"\n            )\n            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n\n        # Verify final arithmetic\n        total_debits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"debit\")\n        total_credits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"credit\")\n        assert ledger.consumed == total_debits\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == total_credits\n        assert ledger.available() == 200 - total_debits + total_credits\n\n    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_launch(self):\n        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_allocation allows launch.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=5, minimum_allocation=5)\n        assert ledger.can_launch() is True\n\n    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_remediate(self):\n        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_remediation_budget allows remediation.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=3, minimum_remediation_budget=3)\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is True\n\n    def test_budget_decay_rate_08(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify budget decay math at rate=0.8: net cost per passing task = 4 turns (spec §4.1).\"\"\"\n        import math\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8)\n        # Simulate one task: 8 turns consumed, then credit floor(8 * 0.8) = 6\n        ledger.debit(8)\n        reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n        assert reimbursed == 6\n        ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n        # Also add 2 overhead turns\n        ledger.debit(2)\n        # Net cost = 8 + 2 - 6 = 4\n        assert ledger.consumed == 10  # 8 + 2\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == 6\n        assert ledger.available() == 200 - 10 + 6  # = 196\n\n    def test_max_sustainable_tasks_at_08(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify sustainability boundary: budget=200, rate=0.8, net_cost=4/task → ~50 tasks (spec §4.2).\"\"\"\n        import math\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(\n            initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8, minimum_allocation=8\n        )\n        task_count = 0\n        while ledger.can_launch():\n            ledger.debit(8)\n            reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n            ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n            ledger.debit(2)  # overhead\n            task_count += 1\n            if task_count > 100:  # safety bound\n                break\n        # 200 / 4 net cost = 50 total capacity, but can_launch requires minimum_allocation=8\n        # After 49 tasks: available = 200 - 49*10 + 49*6 = 4 < 8, so loop exits at 49\n        assert task_count == 49\n        assert ledger.available() == 4\n\n    def test_46_task_sprint_sustainability(self):\n        \"\"\"NFR-003: a 46-task sprint at rate=0.8, budget=200 completes with 16-turn margin.\"\"\"\n        import math\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, reimbursement_rate=0.8)\n        for _ in range(46):\n            ledger.debit(8)\n            reimbursed = math.floor(8 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n            ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n            ledger.debit(2)  # overhead\n        assert ledger.available() > 0\n        # Expected: 200 - 46*(8+2) + 46*6 = 200 - 460 + 276 = 16\n        assert ledger.available() == 16\n\n    def test_budget_exhaustion_property(self):\n        \"\"\"NFR-008: for rate < 1.0, budget monotonically decays and reaches 0 in finite steps.\"\"\"\n        import math\n        import random\n\n        rng = random.Random(42)  # deterministic seed per R-005\n        for _ in range(20):  # 20 trials\n            budget = rng.randint(50, 500)\n            rate = round(rng.uniform(0.1, 0.99), 2)\n            turns_per_task = rng.randint(1, 50)\n            ledger = TurnLedger(\n                initial_budget=budget,\n                reimbursement_rate=rate,\n                minimum_allocation=turns_per_task,\n            )\n\n            prev_available = ledger.available()\n            steps = 0\n            while ledger.can_launch() and steps < 10000:\n                ledger.debit(turns_per_task)\n                reimbursed = math.floor(turns_per_task * rate)\n                ledger.credit(reimbursed)\n                # Verify monotonic decay: each cycle net cost > 0\n                assert ledger.available() < prev_available, (\n                    f\"Budget did not decrease: {prev_available} -> {ledger.available()} \"\n                    f\"(rate={rate}, turns={turns_per_task})\"\n                )\n                prev_available = ledger.available()\n                steps += 1\n            # Budget must have been exhausted in finite steps\n            assert not ledger.can_launch(), (\n                f\"Budget never exhausted after {steps} steps\"\n            )\n\n    def test_rate_boundary_validation(self):\n        \"\"\"SC-001: verify TurnLedger behavior at rate boundary values.\"\"\"\n        import math\n\n        # rate=0.0 → zero reimbursement, valid\n        ledger_zero = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=0.0)\n        ledger_zero.debit(10)\n        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_zero.reimbursement_rate)\n        ledger_zero.credit(reimbursed)\n        assert reimbursed == 0\n        assert ledger_zero.available() == 90  # no reimbursement\n\n        # rate=0.99 → high reimbursement, valid\n        ledger_high = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=0.99)\n        ledger_high.debit(10)\n        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_high.reimbursement_rate)\n        ledger_high.credit(reimbursed)\n        assert reimbursed == 9\n        assert ledger_high.available() == 99\n\n        # rate=1.0 → no decay, net cost = 0 per task (infinite budget)\n        # SC-001 says this should be rejected, but TurnLedger currently has no validation.\n        # This test documents the current behavior: rate=1.0 is accepted but causes zero net cost.\n        ledger_one = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=1.0)\n        ledger_one.debit(10)\n        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_one.reimbursement_rate)\n        ledger_one.credit(reimbursed)\n        assert reimbursed == 10\n        assert ledger_one.available() == 100  # no decay — budget never depletes\n\n        # rate=-0.1 → negative reimbursement (penalizes)\n        # SC-001 says this should be rejected, but TurnLedger currently has no validation.\n        # This test documents the current behavior: negative rate produces negative credits.\n        ledger_neg = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, reimbursement_rate=-0.1)\n        ledger_neg.debit(10)\n        reimbursed = math.floor(10 * ledger_neg.reimbursement_rate)\n        # floor(10 * -0.1) = floor(-1.0) = -1\n        assert reimbursed == -1\n        # credit(-1) should raise ValueError per existing validation\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"non-negative\"):\n            ledger_neg.credit(reimbursed)\n\n\n# m in GateOutcome} == expected\n\n    def test_values(self):\n        assert GateOutcome.PASS.value == \"pass\"\n        assert GateOutcome.FAIL.value == \"fail\"\n        assert Multi-line exemptions require the comment on each line.\n    14→\n    15→**API Impact on `obligation_scanner.py`**:\n    16→- `scan_obligations()` must check each line containing a scaffold match for a trailing `# obligation-exempt` comment.\n    17→- Exempt obligations are still recorded in the `Obligation` dataclass but with an `exempt=True` field (or equivalent filtering).\n    18→- `ObligationReport.undischarged_count` excludes exempt obligations.\n    19→\n    20→---\n    21→\n    22→## OQ-004: MEDIUM Severity vs STRICT Gate Blocking\n    23→\n    24→**Decision**: Separate `medium_severity_obligations` frontmatter field, excluded from gate blocking.\n    25→\n    26→**Resolution**: Scaffold terms found inside code blocks (backtick-fenced) are demoted to MEDIUM severity. MEDIUM severity obligations are stored in a separate frontmatter field (`medium_severity_obligations`) and are NOT counted toward the `undischarged_obligations` field that the ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE checks. The gate blocks only on HIGH severity (non-code-block) undischarged obligations.\n    27→\n    28→**API Impact on `obligation_scanner.py`**:\n    29→- `Obligation` dataclass includes a `severity` field: `\"HIGH\"` (default) or `\"MEDIUM\"` (code-block context).\n    30→- `ObligationReport` exposes `undischarged_count` property that returns count where `discharged=False AND severity != \"MEDIUM\"`.\n    31→- Code-block detection: scaffold terms within ``` fenced blocks are tagged MEDIUM.\n    32→\n    33→---\n    34→\n    35→## OQ-005: D-03/D-04 Coexistence (Defense-in-Depth)\n    36→\n    37→**Decision**: Both gates evaluate independently per NFR-010.\n    38→\n    39→**Resolution**: The existing `WIRING_GATE` (D-03) and the new `ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE` (D-04) operate as independent pipeline gates. Both evaluate on every run. Neither short-circuits the other. If either fails, the pipeline halts. This is defense-in-depth: the wiring gate checks structural artifact presence while the anti-instinct gate checks semantic content quality.\n    40→\n    41→**API Impact**:\n    42→- `ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE` is inserted into `ALL_GATES` between `merge` and `test-strategy` (Phase 2 work).\n    43→- No modifications to `WIRING_GATE` behavior or evaluation order.\n    44→- Both gates remain in `ALL_GATES` list and both are evaluated by the pipeline runner.\n    45→\n    46→---\n    47→\n    48→## OQ-009: Global vs Contract-Specific Pattern Matching\n    49→\n    50→**Decision**: Phase 1 uses global pattern matching; contract-specific matching deferred to future iteration."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e4b79c92-efe6-439a-8057-fa8d1eeb7b4c","uuid":"10c0fe3c-be16-4377-8f47-505814ebf749","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:13:12.942Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.1_Anti-instincts__/artifacts/D-0001/spec.md","content":"# Architecture Decision Record — v3.1 Anti-Instinct Gate Day-1 Decisions\n\n**Sprint**: v3.1_Anti-instincts__\n**Task**: T01.01\n**Source**: Roadmap Section 1.0 Recommended Resolutions\n\n---\n\n## OQ-003: `# obligation-exempt` Syntax Scope\n\n**Decision**: Per-line scope on the scaffold term's line.\n\n**Resolution**: The `# obligation-exempt` comment applies only to the line on which it appears. If a scaffold term (e.g., \"mocked\") appears on a line ending with `# obligation-exempt`, that specific obligation is excluded from the undischarged count. Multi-line exemptions require the comment on each line.\n\n**API Impact on `obligation_scanner.py`**:\n- `scan_obligations()` must check each line containing a scaffold match for a trailing `# obligation-exempt` comment.\n- Exempt obligations are still recorded in the `Obligation` dataclass but with an `exempt=True` field (or equivalent filtering).\n- `ObligationReport.undischarged_count` excludes exempt obligations.\n\n---\n\n## OQ-004: MEDIUM Severity vs STRICT Gate Blocking\n\n**Decision**: Separate `medium_severity_obligations` frontmatter field, excluded from gate blocking.\n\n**Resolution**: Scaffold terms found inside code blocks (backtick-fenced) are demoted to MEDIUM severity. MEDIUM severity obligations are stored in a separate frontmatter field (`medium_severity_obligations`) and are NOT counted toward the `undischarged_obligations` field that the ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE checks. The gate blocks only on HIGH severity (non-code-block) undischarged obligations.\n\n**API Impact on `obligation_scanner.py`**:\n- `Obligation` dataclass includes a `severity` field: `\"HIGH\"` (default) or `\"MEDIUM\"` (code-block context).\n- `ObligationReport` exposes `undischarged_count` property that returns count where `discharged=False AND severity != \"MEDIUM\"`.\n- Code-block detection: scaffold terms within ``` fenced blocks are tagged MEDIUM.\n\n---\n\n## OQ-005: D-03/D-04 Coexistence (Defense-in-Depth)\n\n**Decision**: Both gates evaluate independently per NFR-010.\n\n**Resolution**: The existing `WIRING_GATE` (D-03) and the new `ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE` (D-04) operate as independent pipeline gates. Both evaluate on every run. Neither short-circuits the other. If either fails, the pipeline halts. This is defense-in-depth: the wiring gate checks structural artifact presence while the anti-instinct gate checks semantic content quality.\n\n**API Impact**:\n- `ANTI_INSTINCT_GATE` is inserted into `ALL_GATES` between `merge` and `test-strategy` (Phase 2 work).\n- No modifications to `WIRING_GATE` behavior or evaluation order.\n- Both gates remain in `ALL_GATES` list and both are evaluated by the pipeline runner.\n\n---\n\n## OQ-009: Global vs Contract-Specific Pattern Matching\n\n**Decision**: Phase 1 uses global pattern matching; contract-specific matching deferred to future iteration.","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":69}}}

GateDisplayState(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Visual state for gate status in the TUI phase table.\n\n    Maps gate lifecycle stages to distinct display properties (color, icon, label)\n    for at-a-glance feedback on trailing gate progress.\n\n    Valid transitions:\n        NONE → CHECKING → PASS\n        NONE → CHECKING → FAIL_DEFERRED → REMEDIATING → REMEDIATED\n        NONE → CHECKING → FAIL_DEFERRED → REMEDIATING → HALT\n    \"\"\"\n\n    NONE = \"none\"\n    CHECKING = \"checking\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    FAIL_DEFERRED = \"fail_deferred\"\n    REMEDIATING = \"remediating\"\n   @property\n    def icon(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Short icon/label for TUI column rendering.\"\"\"\n        return _GATE_DISPLAY_ICONS[self]\n\n    @property\n    def label(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Human-readable label for reports.\"\"\"\n        return _GATE_DISPLAY_LABELS[self]\n\n\n# Valid transitions: frozenset of (from_state, to_state) pairs\nGATE_DISPLAY_TRANSITIONS: Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    initial_budget: int\n    consumed: int = 0\n    reimbursed: int = 0\n    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.8\n    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n\n    def available(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n\n    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.consumed += turns\n\n    def credit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reimburse turns to the budget.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"credit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.reimbursed += turns\n\n    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n\n    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for remediation.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_remediation_budget\n\n\ndef build_resume_output(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    halt_task_id: str,\n    remaining_tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    diagnostic_path: str | None = None,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build actionable HALT output with resume command and context.\n\n    Produces an actionable resume block containing:\n    - 1] + s[mid]) / 2\n        return s[mid]\n\n    @property\n    def p95_latency_ms(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"95th percentile gate evaluation latency in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n        if not self.latency_ms:\n            return 0.0\n        s = sorted(self.latency_ms)\n        idx = int(len(s) * 0.95)\n        return s[min(idx, len(s) - 1)]\n\n    def record(self, passed: bool, evaluation_ms: float) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a single shadow gate result.\"\"\"\n        self.total_evaluated += 1\n        if passed:\n            self.passed += 1\n        else:\n            self.failed += 1\n        self.latency_ms.append(evaluation_ms)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":319,"oldLines":6,"newStart":319,"newLines":10,"lines":["     # Wiring gate mode: controls post-task wiring analysis behavior","     # off=disabled, shadow=log only, soft=warn on critical, full=block on critical+major","     wiring_gate_mode: Literal[\"off\", \"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\"] = \"soft\"","+    # Anti-instinct gate rollout mode: controls anti-instinct gate behavior in sprint","+    # off=evaluate+ignore, shadow=evaluate+record, soft=evaluate+record+credit/remediate,","+    # full=evaluate+record+credit/remediate+fail","+    gate_rollout_mode: Literal[\"off\", \"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\"] = \"off\""," ","     def __post_init__(self):","         # Sync release_dir to PipelineConfig.work_dir so both access paths"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

    prompt = (\n            f\"REMEDIATION: Fix the following gate failure for step '{gate_result.step_id}'.\\n\"\n            f\"Failure reason: {gate_result.failure_reason or 'Unknown'}\\n\"\n
